description: We were riding our bikes on the bike path along the Poudre south of the Eastman Koday property. When we rounded the corner we saw one great egret feeding with four great blue herons. The difference in the species was striking. The bird had a long yellow bill, long feathery tail plumes and black legs and feet. This bird was feeding rather voraciously by reaching forward and out into the mud. The herons were passive nearby. This bird was not quite as tall as the herons and more slender. The neck was longer. We rode on a little more. When we came back by the area we saw a second egret with the group. It was also walking and feeding in the mud.
